build.details: Build completed successfully with pre-existing warnings in other blocks. No new errors introduced.

lint.details: Only pre-existing warning in WideSocialMediaItem.tsx for experimental API usage (__experimentalLinkControl). No new lint issues introduced by the changes.

warnings.message: Usage of __experimentalLinkControl from @wordpress/block-editor is not allowed
warnings.note: Pre-existing, not introduced by this change

registration.details: Both parent and child block.json files are correctly configured. Parent provides context via providesContext, child receives via usesContext. All required fields present, attributes properly typed.
validations: showLabel attribute: type boolean, default true
Parent attribute added correctly to child block.json

attributes.details: showLabel attribute properly defined in parent block.json with correct type (boolean) and default (true). Context properly configured and passed to child blocks.

tests.evidence: Section 4 (index 4) and Section 7 (index 7) render with null heading. Nav is properly right-aligned via md:ml-auto.
Section 3 has heading 'Follow Us on All of Our Social Media Platforms and Stay Connected' (66 chars). Text wraps properly without breaking layout.
Section 5 has only 1 child (childCount: 1). Renders correctly without layout issues.
Section 2 has 8 children. Flex wrapping works correctly across breakpoints.
Section 6 has heading and 4 links with labels.found: false. Labels are hidden, title attributes present.
Section 7 has no heading and 3 links with labels.found: false. Both fixes work together: right-alignment + hidden labels.
Section 8 has showLabel: true explicitly set. Labels display normally (labels.found: true), confirming default behavior unchanged.

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements. Block is purely presentational (links with hover states).

checks.evidence: Uses <aside> for block wrapper, <nav> for links container with aria-label, <ul>/<li> for link list structure.
All links are standard <a> elements, fully keyboard accessible. No custom interactive components.

checks.check: ARIA labels present when showLabel false
checks.evidence: Verified via curl: all links in 'Labels Hidden' sections have aria-label attributes (e.g., 'Follow us on Facebook', 'Follow us on Instagram'). Screen readers have access to link purpose even when visual label is hidden.
Verified via curl: links in showLabel=false sections have title attributes (e.g., title='Facebook'). Provides hover tooltip as specified in requirements.

checks.check: Screen reader text for new tab
checks.evidence: PHP template includes sr-only '(opens in new tab)' text when opensInNewTab is true. Verified in page_audit link text data.
Uses h2 for section heading. Appropriate hierarchy for a component block (not page title).

markupDuplication.details: No duplicated markup for responsive behavior. Layout uses standard Tailwind responsive classes (md:flex-row, md:ml-auto, etc.) without duplicating DOM elements.

tsxPhpSync.details: TSX and PHP are properly synchronized

checks.evidence: grep for '$attributes[' returned no results. Both PHP files use auto-extracted variables correctly.
TSX line 77: className='shrink-0 relative md:ml-auto'. PHP line 30: class='shrink-0 md:ml-auto'. Classes match (relative is editor-only).
WideSocialMediaItem.php line 26: $showLabel = $block->context['takt/wide-social-media/showLabel'] ?? true. Correctly reads from context with default.
TSX uses opacity-50 when !showLabel (editor visual feedback). PHP uses <?php if ( $showLabel && !empty($label) ) : ?> to conditionally render span. Logic is synced.

Both files prioritize label over link.title for title attribute. TSX line 153-155: title={!showLabel ? (attributes.label || attributes.link?.title || '') : undefined}. PHP line 29: $titleAttr = !$showLabel ? ($label ?: $linkTitle) : '';. Logic matches.
Both PHP files have proper @var docblocks for all attributes. WideSocialMedia.php includes @var bool $showLabel. WideSocialMediaItem.php lists all attributes with types.

tests.evidence: allowedBlocks: ['takt/wide-social-media-item'] in TSX. Child block has parent: ['takt/wide-social-media'] in block.json. Restriction enforced bidirectionally.
Template includes one default item: [['takt/wide-social-media-item']]. New blocks start with one item as expected.
All 9 test page sections render inner block content correctly. Child counts: [2, 4, 8, 3, 3, 1, 4, 3, 3].

specCompliance.details: All implementation requirements from planning spec verified

requirements.requirement: Links are right-aligned on desktop when heading is empty
requirements.evidence: md:ml-auto class present on all nav elements (verified via curl). Sections 4 and 7 have no heading and nav is still right-aligned in screenshots.

requirements.requirement: Links are right-aligned on desktop when heading is present (no regression)
requirements.evidence: Sections with heading (0-3, 5-6, 8) all show nav aligned right. md:justify-between on parent + md:ml-auto on nav work together correctly.

requirements.requirement: Mobile layout stacks correctly regardless of heading presence
requirements.evidence: 375px screenshots show vertical stacking (flex-col on parent div). No horizontal overflow or layout issues.

requirements.requirement: showLabel=true shows label text (default behavior unchanged)
requirements.evidence: Section 8 (explicit showLabel: true) has labels.found: true. Sections 0-5 (default/omitted) all have labels.found: true. Default behavior confirmed.

requirements.requirement: showLabel=false hides label text on frontend
requirements.evidence: Sections 6 and 7 (showLabel: false) have labels.found: false in page_audit results. No span.text-xs elements rendered.

requirements.requirement: showLabel=false adds title attribute to <a> element with label value
requirements.evidence: curl check found title='Facebook', title='Instagram' etc. in Labels Hidden sections. Title attribute is populated with label value.

requirements.requirement: Hovering over icon-only link shows tooltip with social media name
requirements.evidence: Title attributes present. Browser will show native tooltip on hover (standard HTML behavior).

requirements.requirement: Accessibility: aria-label still present on links when showLabel is false
requirements.evidence: curl check in Labels Hidden sections found aria-label='Follow us on Facebook', aria-label='Follow us on Instagram' etc. All links maintain aria-label regardless of showLabel.

requirements.requirement: Editor shows label with reduced opacity when showLabel is false
requirements.evidence: TSX line 180: className includes 'opacity-50': !showLabel. Visual feedback present in editor (not testable on frontend, but code verified).
TSX includes InspectorControls with ToggleControl for showLabel. Test page variations prove it works (sections 6-8 have different showLabel values, rendered correctly).

passedChecks: Build compiles successfully without errors
No new lint issues introduced
Block registration is correct (parent and child)
showLabel attribute properly defined and passed via context
All edge cases handled (empty heading, long heading, single item, many items, showLabel variations)
Semantic HTML structure with proper ARIA labels
aria-label attributes present on all links regardless of showLabel
Title attributes added to links when showLabel is false
Screen reader text for 'opens in new tab'
No attribute extraction anti-pattern in PHP
md:ml-auto class present in both TSX and PHP
Context reading works correctly in child block
Conditional label rendering synced between TSX and PHP
Title attribute logic synced between TSX and PHP
Proper @var docblocks in PHP files
Inner blocks configuration works correctly
All 10 QA checklist items from planning spec verified
Test page has 9 block instances as expected
Right-alignment fix works with and without heading
showLabel toggle works correctly
